title: Need better documentation on cleaning up bad podman installations
body:
I had an old version of Podman installed, then I tried installing DockerDesktop, but it failed thanks to podman.  Then I uninstalled old podman tried Docker Desktop again.  Then I uninstalled DockerDesktop, then installed latest podman.  The podman desktop failed because it wanted to install on hypervisor machine.  I used the podman command line install and it said I needed to set up hypervisor.  

So after several iterations of this I got podman to install, I then saw it had a podman-machine-default hypervisor vm running using Get-VM, I used Remove-VM to remove the hypervisor to try to get to a fresh blank slate.

Finally I thought I was in a good place, when I ran podman-machine-init.  It went through the process and sad Machine init complete to start your machine run podman machine start.

So I ran that command and it said "Error: could not start api proxy since expected pipe is not available: podman-machine-default"

PS C:\Users\steve> podman machine list
NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED        LAST UP     CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
podman-machine-default*  hyperv      2 minutes ago  Never       4           2GiB        100GiB

PS C:\Users\steve> podman system connection list
Name                         URI                                                         Identity
                                Default     ReadWrite
podman-machine-default       ssh://core@127.0.0.1:6326/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock  C:\Users\steve\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\machine  true        true
podman-machine-default-root  ssh://root@127.0.0.1:6326/run/podman/podman.sock            C:\Users\steve\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\machine  false       true

So, demoralized,  basically right now I just want to go in find all the files, registry entries, system settings and any other system change that Podman makes and purge every single thing that Podman creates from the system but none of Podman's clean up scripts remove all the pieces and I am blocked from using both Podman and docker Desktop and cannot develop using containers on this system any longer :-(

Ideally I could run podman, on my machine and start doing actual development with containers.  But that may be too much to hope for at this point.

Your documentation does not adequately explain the internals of how pipes are used so that it is possible to understand how to troubleshoot issues when things fail to function as expected.  What for example is Podman looking for when it is trying to find the "expected pipe" called podman-machine-default?  Where is this? How do we confirm its state and status?

The following are other commands I have run to try to make some sense out of the issue:

```bash
PS C:\Users\steve\.config\containers\podman> Get-VM

Name                   State CPUUsage(%) MemoryAssigned(M) Uptime   Status             Version
----                   ----- ----------- ----------------- ------   ------             -------
Buildabear2            Off   0           0                 00:00:00 Operating normally 11.0
podman-machine-default Off   0           0                 00:00:00 Operating normally 11.0


PS C:\Users\steve\.config\containers\podman> Start-VM podman-machine-default
PS C:\Users\steve\.config\containers\podman> podman system connection list
Name                         URI                                                          Identity
              Default     ReadWrite
podman-machine-default       ssh://user@localhost:51646/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock  C:\Users\steve\.ssh\podman-machine-default  false       false
podman-machine-default-root  ssh://root@localhost:51646/run/podman/podman.sock            C:\Users\steve\.ssh\podman-machine-default  true        false
PS C:\Users\steve\.config\containers\podman> podman system connection list
Name                         URI                                                          Identity
              Default     ReadWrite
podman-machine-default       ssh://user@localhost:51646/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock  C:\Users\steve\.ssh\podman-machine-default  false       false
podman-machine-default-root  ssh://root@localhost:51646/run/podman/podman.sock            C:\Users\steve\.ssh\podman-machine-default  true        false
PS C:\Users\steve\.config\containers\podman> podman system connection
Manage remote API service destinations

Description:
  Manage remote API service destination information in podman configuration

Usage:
  podman.exe system connection [command]

Available Commands:
  add         Record destination for the Podman service
  default     Set named destination as default
  list        List destination for the Podman service(s)
  remove      Delete named destination
  rename      Rename "old" to "new"

Error: missing command 'podman.exe system connection COMMAND'
PS C:\Users\steve\.config\containers\podman> podman system connection default podman-machine-default
Error: "podman-machine-default" destination is not defined. See "podman system connection add ..." to create a connection
PS C:\Users\steve\.config\containers\podman> podman system connection default podman-machine-default
Error: "podman-machine-default" destination is not defined. See "podman system connection add ..." to create a connection

```


Couple things I have observed:
1. podman system connection default, and remove seem to have absolutely zero effect yet return no sort of error message when run.
2. I can start the podman VM using Start-VM, but podman machine start can't seem to find a pipe (whatever that is) to the Hypevisor to do the start/stop.

comments.body:
did you try `podman machine reset` ? I think that will largely do what you wanted.

edit: this somewhat depends on what files are left in the messed up installation. from the report, i cannot tell definitively
Thanks I will give it a try.
I think that might fix the issue.  But I can't be sure.  I uninstalled and re-installed things many times until suddenly things are working
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing as `podman machine reset` should be the fix.
